question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: mempoolAPIService] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that mempoolAPIService has no reference to an account repository or signer database to confirm that the mempool layer cannot look up the required signers for any account, making it structurally impossible to enforce owner-approval checks even if the endpoints were implemented?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Mempool() never validates the blockchain_identifier in the NetworkRequest to submit requests for a blockchain they are not authorized on, bypassing chain-specific signer requirements?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MempoolTransaction() never validates the hash field of the transaction_identifier to submit a request with a crafted transaction hash that collides with a legitimate pending transaction, potentially hijacking the authorization flow?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Mempool() never enforces any caller authentication to confirm that the /mempool endpoint is a completely open, unauthenticated surface, and then use this as a stepping stone to demonstrate that the entire transaction authorization pipeline lacks owner-approval enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: NewMempoolAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NewMempoolAPIService() creates a new mempoolAPIService without registering any signer-approval callbacks or hooks to confirm that no event-driven authorization check will ever fire when a transaction enters the mempool, allowing unauthorized transactions to proceed undetected?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Mempool() never checks whether the caller's IP or TLS certificate corresponds to a registered signer to submit mempool queries from any network location without proving ownership of any account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MempoolTransaction() never checks whether the caller is the payer or a required signer of the requested transaction to retrieve authorization status for transactions they do not own, enabling targeted monitoring and manipulation of other users' pending transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Mempool() returns ErrNotImplemented regardless of the caller's identity to confirm that the system treats all callers identically with respect to mempool access, meaning there is no privileged signer path that enforces owner approval at this layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: mempoolAPIService] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that mempoolAPIService{} is instantiated as a pointer receiver (*mempoolAPIService) with no initialization of any authorization state to confirm that the zero-value struct is the only state ever used, meaning no signer registry or owner map is ever populated?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Mempool() never checks the transaction count or pending-transaction list to confirm that the system has no mechanism to detect when a transaction with insufficient signatures is sitting in the mempool, allowing it to be submitted and executed without full owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MempoolTransaction() never returns a list of required signers for a pending transaction to prevent co-signers from knowing they need to approve a transaction, allowing a single party to push through a multi-sig transaction without notifying other owners?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Mempool() never enforces a minimum-signature threshold check to submit a transaction that has only one of N required signatures, knowing the mempool layer will never reject it for insufficient signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: NewMempoolAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NewMempoolAPIService() is a pure factory function with no side effects (no registration with an authorization service, no signer enrollment) to confirm that the mempool service lifecycle is completely decoupled from the authorization infrastructure?
